title: dactylis glomerata subsp. nestorii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. nestorii, also known as Nestor's grass or a subspecies of the wild meadow grass (Dactylis glomerata), is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations, due to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which provide a range of health benefits such as soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Dactylis glomerata subsp. nestorii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and support overall well-being.
